results.why: This pass tightens [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] around cleaner four-color fixing, stronger planeswalker/proliferate support, and a smoother casual curve. The land swaps upgrade color consistency, while the new utility spells add protection, interaction, and better payoff for counters and walkers. It also removes a card outside Atraxa's color identity and trims some slower or less aligned draw and ramp pieces.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.72.
results.why: This pass lowers the deck's average cost by replacing several expensive finishers, slow ramp pieces, and excess basics with cheaper ramp, interaction, and proliferate support. [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] wants early board development, so the swaps favor two-mana rocks, efficient cantrips, and lands that fix colors cleanly. The result is a smoother curve with more plays in the early turns while still supporting counters and planeswalkers.

raw.plan: This pass tightens [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] around faster fixing, cheaper ramp, and more efficient interaction while keeping the deck in a casual optimized range. The added fetch lands and triomes make the four-color mana base smoother than excess basics and slow tap-fixers. Low-cost counters, scalable rocks, and efficient value pieces also improve consistency without pushing the deck too far.
raw.why: This pass tightens [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] around faster fixing, cheaper ramp, and more efficient interaction while keeping the deck in a casual optimized range. The added fetch lands and triomes make the four-color mana base smoother than excess basics and slow tap-fixers. Low-cost counters, scalable rocks, and efficient value pieces also improve consistency without pushing the deck too far.

added.astral cornucopia: [[Astral Cornucopia]] works especially well with [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] proliferating charge counters.
added.demonic tutor: [[Demonic Tutor]] is a clean upgrade for finding the deck's best card when needed.
added.dovin's veto: [[Dovin's Veto]] gives cheap, reliable protection against key noncreature spells.
added.everflowing chalice: [[Everflowing Chalice]] scales well early and gets better with [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] proliferate.
added.fellwar stone: [[Fellwar Stone]] is efficient two-mana fixing for a four-color Commander deck.
added.flooded strand: [[Flooded Strand]] improves color access and pairs well with the added triomes.
added.indatha triome: [[Indatha Triome]] provides three colors at once and is fetchable when fixing is needed.
added.marsh flats: [[Marsh Flats]] increases mana consistency by finding key dual-typed lands.
added.misty rainforest: [[Misty Rainforest]] helps assemble the right colors more efficiently than basic-heavy draws.
added.polluted delta: [[Polluted Delta]] adds another strong fetch to smooth early development.
added.raffine's tower: [[Raffine's Tower]] is strong three-color fixing that can be fetched when necessary.
added.spara's headquarters: [[Spara's Headquarters]] broadens access to core colors while supporting fetch-land sequencing.
added.swan song: [[Swan Song]] is a very efficient way to stop problematic enchantments, instants, and sorceries.
added.teferi's talent: [[Teferi's Talent]] adds efficient value that fits the deck's more streamlined plan.
added.tezzeret's gambit: [[Tezzeret's Gambit]] draws cards while adding proliferate synergy for [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]].
added.windswept heath: [[Windswept Heath]] improves fixing by turning into the colors the hand is missing.
added.zagoth triome: [[Zagoth Triome]] is another fetchable three-color land that stabilizes the mana base.

removed.island: Extra [[Island]] copies are cut because the upgraded mana base covers blue more efficiently.
removed.forest: A surplus [[Forest]] is less necessary once fetch lands and triomes improve fixing.
removed.plains: Basic [[Plains]] is trimmed to make room for stronger multicolor sources.
removed.swamp: Basic [[Swamp]] is reduced because the new lands supply black more flexibly.
removed.blue sun's zenith: [[Blue Sun's Zenith]] is mana-intensive compared with the deck's newer value options.
removed.commander's sphere: [[Commander's Sphere]] is slower than the cheaper ramp pieces being added.
removed.dovin baan: [[Dovin Baan]] is lower-impact than the more efficient interaction and value upgrades.
removed.evolving wilds: [[Evolving Wilds]] is outclassed by the stronger fetch lands added here.
removed.nicol bolas, dragon-god: [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] falls outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] color identity and must be removed.
removed.opal palace: [[Opal Palace]] is less important than cleaner four-color fixing.
removed.pull from tomorrow: [[Pull from Tomorrow]] is another expensive draw spell that is less efficient for this build.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] provides utility but does not help the deck cast its spells on time.
removed.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] is cut for being a costly card-draw effect.
removed.terramorphic expanse: [[Terramorphic Expanse]] is replaced by faster and more flexible fetch-based fixing.

raw.plan: This pass shifts the deck toward more reliable interaction by adding flexible answers to creatures, artifacts, enchantments, and problematic permanents. The added removal stays mostly on-plan for casual play and helps cover multiple permanent types without pushing the budget too hard.
